text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a software development practice where team members frequently combine their code changes into a shared repository. Each time new code is added, a CI pipeline automatically kicks off an automated build to compile the project. Additionally, the system runs a suite of tests, such as unit and integration tests, to verify the new code. The main benefit of this process is catching issues early—often within minutes of the change. If a test fails or the build breaks, developers are quickly alerted, allowing them to fix the problem before it becomes larger. This leads to higher-quality software and smoother collaboration.
